seo.title: E-Commerce Data Extraction AI Agent Checklist
seo.description: Production readiness checklist for data extraction AI agents in e-commerce. Covers FTC advertising guidelines, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS for payments.

content.intro: Deploying AI agents for data extraction in e-commerce carries significant regulatory and financial risk — inaccurate product claims, pricing errors, or misrepresented return policies can trigger FTC enforcement, CCPA/GDPR violations, and direct revenue loss. Schema-guided LLM extraction must be rigorously validated to ensure every extracted field meets legal accuracy standards before it reaches customers or downstream systems. This checklist provides concrete, actionable controls to ensure your extraction pipeline is compliant, auditable, and production-ready.

sections.heading: Schema Design & Field Validation
sections.description: Ensure your extraction schema is purpose-built for e-commerce data accuracy, with strict type enforcement and field-level validation rules that prevent malformed or legally non-compliant data from propagating downstream.

items.label: Define strict JSON schema with enum constraints for product condition fields
items.description: Product condition values (e.g., 'new', 'refurbished', 'used') must be constrained to an approved enum list to prevent fabricated or ambiguous claims that violate FTC substantiation requirements.

items.label: Enforce numeric type validation on all price and discount fields
items.description: Price fields must be validated as positive decimals with currency codes; reject any extraction where price is a string, null, or negative to prevent incorrect pricing from reaching checkout flows.

items.label: Require completeness checks on legally mandated fields before schema acceptance
items.description: Fields such as return window (days), refund eligibility, and shipping restrictions must be marked as required in the schema; incomplete records must be flagged for human review rather than silently passed.

items.label: Implement regex pattern validation on SKU, UPC, and ASIN identifiers
items.description: Product identifiers must match known format patterns to prevent hallucinated or transposed codes that cause inventory mismatches and customer-facing errors.

items.label: Add cross-field consistency rules (e.g., sale price must be less than original price)
items.description: Define post-extraction validation rules that check logical relationships between fields to catch LLM errors that pass individual field checks but violate business logic.

items.label: Version-control all extraction schemas and log schema version with each extraction run
items.description: Schema changes must be tracked with semantic versioning so that downstream data consumers and audit logs can trace which schema version produced a given extracted record.

items.label: Define explicit null-handling policy for optional fields in schema documentation
items.description: Distinguish between 'field not present in source' and 'field present but empty' to prevent null values from being interpreted as zero prices or unlimited return windows.

sections.heading: LLM Extraction Accuracy & Hallucination Controls
sections.description: Structured output extraction via LLMs introduces hallucination risk on high-stakes fields like pricing, product claims, and policy terms. These controls reduce the probability of fabricated data entering your e-commerce pipeline.

items.label: Use grounded extraction prompts that instruct the model to return null rather than infer missing values
items.description: Explicitly instruct the LLM in the system prompt: 'If a field value is not present in the source document, return null. Do not infer, estimate, or generate values.' This prevents fabricated prices or invented return policies.

items.label: Implement confidence scoring or extraction provenance for price and claim fields
items.description: For each extracted price and product claim, require the model to return a source span or character offset from the input document; records without traceable provenance must be quarantined for review.

items.label: Run dual-pass extraction with independent verification prompt for high-risk fields
items.description: For fields like 'return policy days', 'warranty terms', and 'sale price', run a second LLM call that reads the original document and the extracted value and confirms or disputes the extraction.

items.label: Establish per-field hallucination rate benchmarks using a labeled evaluation dataset
items.description: Create a golden dataset of 200+ e-commerce documents with ground-truth extractions; measure per-field accuracy and set minimum thresholds (e.g., price accuracy ≥ 99.5%) before production deployment.

items.label: Set maximum input token limits and chunking strategy for long product catalogs
items.description: Define a chunking strategy that keeps product descriptions within the model's reliable extraction window; excessively long contexts increase field confusion and cross-product data bleed.

items.label: Log all raw LLM outputs alongside final extracted records for audit trail
items.description: Store the unprocessed LLM response JSON for every extraction job so that disputed extractions can be traced back to the model output and differentiated from post-processing errors.

items.label: Monitor extraction drift weekly by re-running evaluation dataset against production model
items.description: LLM providers update models without notice; weekly regression tests on your labeled dataset detect accuracy degradation before it causes pricing errors or policy misrepresentations in production.

sections.heading: Regulatory Compliance & Consumer Protection
sections.description: E-commerce data extraction directly feeds customer-facing content, making regulatory compliance non-negotiable. These controls ensure extracted data meets FTC, CCPA/GDPR, PCI DSS, and ADA requirements before publication.

items.label: Block extraction pipeline from outputting unsubstantiated superlative claims (e.g., '#1', 'best')
items.description: Implement a post-extraction filter that flags or removes superlative marketing claims extracted from vendor documents unless accompanied by an extracted substantiation source, per FTC advertising guidelines.

items.label: Ensure extracted pricing data is never stored in logs with full PAN or payment context
items.description: If extraction pipelines process documents containing payment card data, implement PCI DSS-compliant log scrubbing to ensure card numbers, CVVs, and expiration dates are never written to extraction logs.

items.label: Tag extracted personal data fields (customer names, emails, addresses) with CCPA/GDPR data category labels
items.description: When extracting from documents that may contain personal data, automatically classify and tag PII fields with their legal data category to enable downstream data subject access request (DSAR) fulfillment.

items.label: Validate that extracted return/refund policy terms match the merchant's published policy verbatim
items.description: Run a similarity check between extracted return policy fields and the merchant's canonical policy document; flag discrepancies above a defined threshold for human review before publishing to product pages.

items.label: Implement ADA-compliant alt-text extraction for product images referenced in extracted records
items.description: When extraction schema includes image URLs, trigger a secondary process to generate or validate descriptive alt-text for each image to meet WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility requirements.

items.label: Enforce data minimization by excluding fields not required for the extraction use case
items.description: Configure the extraction schema to explicitly exclude personal data fields not needed for product catalog use cases, reducing CCPA/GDPR exposure and limiting liability in the event of a data breach.

items.label: Maintain extraction audit logs with timestamps and operator IDs for minimum 24 months
items.description: Retain structured logs of every extraction job including input document hash, schema version, operator ID, and output record ID for at least 24 months to support FTC investigation response and GDPR accountability obligations.

items.label: Document legal basis for processing any personal data encountered during extraction
items.description: If extraction pipelines may encounter documents with personal data, document the legal basis (e.g., legitimate interest, contract performance) in your GDPR Records of Processing Activities (RoPA) before deployment.

sections.heading: Monitoring, Alerting & Incident Response
sections.description: Production extraction pipelines require continuous monitoring to detect accuracy degradation, schema violations, and compliance failures before they cause customer harm, refund disputes, or regulatory exposure.

items.label: Set real-time alerts for price extraction anomalies (e.g., price > $10,000 or price = $0)
items.description: Configure threshold-based alerts that fire immediately when extracted prices fall outside category-specific plausible ranges; route alerts to both engineering and merchandising teams for rapid response.

items.label: Implement a dead-letter queue for all extraction records that fail schema validation
items.description: Records that fail type, completeness, or consistency validation must be routed to a dead-letter queue with failure reason codes rather than silently dropped or passed with defaults, enabling systematic error analysis.

items.label: Define and document an incident response runbook for pricing extraction errors
items.description: Create a step-by-step runbook that specifies how to identify affected SKUs, roll back incorrect prices, notify customers who saw incorrect pricing, and document the incident for potential FTC disclosure.

items.label: Track per-field extraction success rate as a KPI in your observability dashboard
items.description: Instrument your extraction pipeline to emit per-field null rates, validation failure rates, and confidence score distributions to a dashboard; set SLO thresholds that trigger alerts when rates degrade.

items.label: Conduct monthly sampling audits where human reviewers validate 50+ randomly selected extraction records
items.description: Assign a QA reviewer to manually compare 50+ extracted records against source documents each month; track accuracy by field type and feed findings back into prompt engineering and schema refinement.

items.label: Log and alert on extraction latency spikes that may indicate model degradation or prompt injection attempts
items.description: Unusual latency patterns can indicate adversarial inputs or model issues; set p95 latency alerts and inspect slow extraction jobs for prompt injection attempts in source documents.

items.label: Establish a customer-facing error correction SLA for reported pricing or policy inaccuracies
items.description: Define a maximum response time (e.g., 2 business hours) for correcting customer-reported extraction errors on live product pages, and track SLA compliance to demonstrate good-faith compliance efforts.

notes.regulation: FTC Advertising Guidelines — 16 CFR Part 255 & Dot Com Disclosures
notes.note: Any product claim extracted and published on your e-commerce platform must be substantiated at the time of publication. AI-extracted claims from vendor documents do not transfer substantiation liability to the vendor — your platform is responsible for ensuring claims are truthful and not misleading. Implement a claim classification step that flags comparative, superlative, and health-related claims for human review before they go live.

notes.regulation: CCPA / GDPR — Data Minimization & Purpose Limitation
notes.note: If your extraction pipeline processes documents that may contain personal data (e.g., customer reviews, order histories, vendor contracts), you must ensure data is collected only for the specified extraction purpose and not retained beyond operational necessity. Under GDPR Article 5(1)(b) and CCPA Section 1798.100, repurposing extracted personal data for profiling or marketing without a separate legal basis constitutes a violation.

Extraction pipelines that process invoices, receipts, or order documents may inadvertently ingest payment card data. PCI DSS Requirement 3 prohibits storing sensitive authentication data post-authorization. Implement automated PAN detection and redaction in your document pre-processing step before any LLM call, and document this control in your PCI DSS scope assessment.

notes.regulation: ADA / WCAG 2.1 AA — Section 508 & State-Level Accessibility Laws
notes.note: E-commerce platforms serving US customers are subject to ADA Title III accessibility requirements, increasingly enforced through litigation. Extracted product data that populates web pages must include accessible attributes — particularly alt-text for images and structured markup for pricing and availability. Failure to include accessible extracted data has been cited in ADA demand letters; ensure your extraction schema includes alt-text and ARIA label fields.

cta.heading: Automate Your E-Commerce Agent Compliance
cta.description: Use Vex to continuously validate your e-commerce extraction pipeline against FTC, CCPA/GDPR, and PCI DSS requirements — catching pricing errors, policy misrepresentations, and schema violations before they reach your customers.
